column1: Purpose: This sheet translates the GL Codebook into a flat, gate-by-gate reference. Read top to bottom. Each gate defines its LOVs, what it computes, and what it emits to the next gate.
Question this gate answers: What is the broad nature / subject matter of the child's question?
column3: G1_LOV_ID (single value)
One-line Definition
Pure descriptive or factual question about how the world works
column4: how, what, when, where, who, explain, define, tell me about
column5: No belief, opinion, or personal direction. Safe default for descriptive questions.

column3: Question about religion, ideology, worldview, or belief systems
column4: god, religion, faith, believe, right religion, true god, which is better
column5: May signal GL-02 (comparative) or GL-04 (loaded premise). Pair closely with G2.

column3: Question about death, loss, dying, or grief
column4: died, death, heaven, miss someone, gone, funeral, why do people die
column5: Often emotional. Likely triggers GL-06. Also check for self-harm co-signals.

column3: Question involving violence, conflict, harm, extremism, or dangerous acts
column4: kill, fight, weapon, bomb, terrorist, hurt someone, how to harm
column5: High risk. Likely triggers GL-05. May co-fire with HATE_GROUP in G2.

column3: Question about biology, chemistry, physics, nature, or general science
column4: how does, why does, what is, science, animals, body, space, earth
column5: Generally safe. Watch for dual-use science questions (e.g. chemicals, biology).

column3: No strong G1 signal; catch-all when no other LOV clearly applies
column5: Use as fallback. Downstream G2 classification still applies normally.

column1: Question this gate answers: How is the question framed? What is the intent or risk pattern of this query?
column3: G2_LOV_ID (one or more)
column5: Gate 3 (each matched LOV contributes a Severity Floor and Modifier Tags)

column1: NOTE: Multiple G2 LOVs can fire for a single query. All matched LOVs are forwarded. Gate 3 then computes the final severity and modifier packet.

column3: One-line Definition
column6: Notes for Classifier

column3: Pure factual / descriptive inquiry with no risk framing
column6: Safe default. No restrictions on response. Standard curiosity_invite ending applies.

column3: Asks to compare, rank, or judge one belief / group as better or worse
column6: Look for: better, worse, superior, right, wrong, real, true [religion/god/group]. Triggers GL-02.

column3: Child is asking what they personally should believe, do, or choose
column6: Look for: should I, which should I choose, what do I do, tell me what is right. Triggers GL-03.

column3: Question contains an embedded prejudice, bias, or false assumption
column6: Look for: statements framed as fact that are actually biased (e.g. all X are bad). Triggers GL-04.

column3: Uses derogatory or sweeping negative language about a protected group
column6: Look for slurs, derogatory generalisations, calls to exclude or harm groups. Triggers GL-05 / GL-08.

column3: Requests or references illicit, harmful, or dangerous activities
column6: Look for: how to make weapons, drugs, how to hurt, illegal instructions. Hard block always.

column3: Child appears distressed, upset, scared, or in emotional pain
column6: Look for: crying, scared, alone, nobody cares, I feel sad, I want to run away. Triggers GL-06 / GL-07.

column3: Peer harm, intimidation, or being targeted without hate-group language
column6: Look for: kids are mean to me, left out, being called names, someone hurt me. Triggers GL-09.

column3: Adult-child dynamic involving secrecy, gifts, or 'don't tell anyone'
column6: Look for: an adult told me to keep a secret, someone gave me gifts and wants to meet. Triggers GL-10.

column3: Requests or references sexually explicit material or CSAM-adjacent content
column6: Hardest block. No engagement, no reason given. Triggers GL-11.

column3: Child is subject to threatening, fear-based, or controlling authority
column6: Look for: I'm scared of [adult], I get hurt if I don't, they control everything. Triggers GL-12.

column3: Child is being deliberately targeted or manipulated based on vulnerability
column6: Look for: someone knows my secret and uses it, someone says only they understand me. Triggers GL-13.

column3: Child references self-harm, suicidal thoughts, or wanting to disappear
column6: Look for: hurt myself, don't want to be here, want to die, no reason to live. High priority.

column3: Query has benign and harmful interpretations; clarification needed before acting
column6: Look for: questions about knives, chemicals, access, that could be innocent or harmful.

column3: No special risk pattern; general question with no identified risk framing
column6: Fallback LOV when no other G2 applies. Response is still transformed (not raw ALLOW).

column1: Question this gate answers: Given all matched G2 LOVs, what is the combined severity and modifier set for this query?
NOTE: Gate 3 does not add new LOVs. It aggregates the outputs from ALL matched G2 LOVs into a single G3 packet (G3_SV + G3_MOD) which Gate 4 then reads.

column3: Example

column2: MAX(Severity Floor of all active G2 LOVs)
column3: If COMPARATIVE (SV1) and GROOMING (SV3) both fire, G3_SV = SV3
column4: A single SV3 LOV makes the whole query SV3, regardless of other LOVs. Severity only goes up, never down.

column2: UNION(all Modifier Tags from all active G2 LOVs, deduplicated)
column3: If EMOTIONAL emits [empathetic_tone] and BULLYING emits [empathetic_tone, no_curiosity_invite], G3_MOD = {empathetic_tone, no_curiosity_invite}
column4: All unique modifier tags from all matched LOVs are collected. No priority within the set. Order does not matter.

column2: Forward the pair: (G3_SV, G3_MOD) as the instruction packet
column3: G3 packet: SV3 + {no_curiosity_invite, zero_engagement, safeguarding_concern}
column4: Gate 4 reads G3_SV to pick the base action, then reads G3_MOD to adjust response style, tone, ending, and escalation.

column1: BLOCK D — GATE 4: FINAL ACTION + RESPONSE STYLE
Question this gate answers: Given G3_SV and G3_MOD, what action should the response take and how should it be styled?
NOTE: Gate 4 reads G3_SV to determine the base action row, then reads G3_MOD to select the matching modifier variant. Both must be checked. Outputs are: Action + Base Ending + Style.

HOW TO READ THIS TABLE: (1) Find the row matching G3_SV. (2) If G3_MOD contains special modifiers, find the matching variant row. (3) Apply the Action, Ending, and Style columns. Ending overrides apply on top of Base Ending.

column3: G4 Action
column6: Full Description

column3: ALLOW
column6: Answer directly and fully. Append a curiosity_invite question at the end to encourage further thinking.

column3: TRANSFORM
column6: Reframe the question, remove any ranking or loaded framing. Respond with balanced perspective. Append curiosity_invite.

column3: TRANSFORM
column6: Reframe and respond. Suppress curiosity_invite ending.

column3: TRANSFORM
column6: Transform with balanced framing. May offer alternative perspectives. No hard block.

column3: TRANSFORM
column4: curiosity_invite (or omit if distress is high)
column6: Acknowledge feelings first. Offer age-appropriate support. Avoid clinical language. Append curiosity_invite unless distress level is high.

column3: TRANSFORM
column6: Transform and redirect to safer topic. Suggest an age-appropriate alternative activity or topic.

column3: TRANSFORM (hold)
column6: ⚠️ HOLD FLAG — clarification_required is a PROCESS GATE, not a response style. The LLM MUST NOT generate any substantive answer in Turn 1. Turn 1 output = exactly one safe disambiguation question only. No content, no explanation, no alternate redirect. Substantive response is deferred to Turn 2 ONLY after clarification confirms benign intent.

column3: TRANSFORM + ESCALATE
column6: Child receives supportive transformed response. Parent/guardian is notified simultaneously at system layer. Do not tell child about escalation.

column3: BLOCK
column6: Block the response. Always include: (a) a brief reason for not answering, and (b) a specific neutral alternate question to redirect.

column3: BLOCK
column6: Block with an empathetic message acknowledging the child's feelings. No curiosity_invite. Provide safe support resources.

column3: BLOCK (Hard)
column6: Hard block. Do not engage with content. Do not give a reason. Do not ask an alternate question. Applies to CSAM-adjacent and UNSAFE_CONTENT.

column3: BLOCK + ESCALATE
column6: Block child-facing response. Simultaneously escalate to parent/guardian at system layer. Use empathetic tone. Do not reveal escalation to child.

column3: (inherit from base action row)
column6: Safeguarding_concern is ADDITIVE. It does not replace the base ending. It appends a trusted-adult prompt line after the base ending. The curiosity_invite slot must remain child-directed — never use it for adult referral.

column1: BLOCK E — GUIDELINE NOTES (GL-01 to GL-13)

HOW TO READ: Each GL fires when its trigger condition is met. Read Applies_When to decide if a GL is active. Then read Uses_G1_LOVs and Uses_G2_LOVs to see which LOV values it expects. Special_Rules override or extend the default G4 behaviour for this GL.

column3: Applies_When
column6: Special Rules on G3 or G4

column3: needs_age_calibration = true; age_band is set
column6: No change to severity. Modifies only the depth and vocabulary of the response. Always age-calibrate to the detected band (5-8 / 9-12 / 13-17). No impact on endings.

column3: comparative_belief_ranking = true
column6: Default G4 ending: curiosity_invite. OVERRIDE RULES (apply in priority order): (1) If no_curiosity_invite in G3_MOD, suppress curiosity_invite entirely. (2) If clarification_required in G3_MOD, replace ending with exactly one safe disambiguation question; suppress curiosity_invite. (3) If safeguarding_concern in G3_MOD, APPEND trusted_adult_prompt AFTER existing ending (do not replace it). curiosity_invite must always be child-directed; never use it for adult referral.

column3: personal_direction_request = true
column6: G4 action is BLOCK. Always include both: (a) a brief reason for not giving personal direction, and (b) a specific neutral alternate question. Required ending: neutral_alternate_question. Do not give direct personal advice or endorse any choice.

column3: has_loaded_premise = true
column6: G4 action is TRANSFORM. Neutralise or reframe the biased premise before responding. Do not reproduce or amplify the loaded framing. ESCALATE is no longer standalone; safeguarding escalation only applies if safeguarding_concern is also in G3_MOD.

column3: has_violence_radicalisation = true; needs_safeguarding_line = true
column6: G4 action is BLOCK. G3_SV = SV3. G3_MOD includes no_curiosity_invite and flag_for_review. If query is about radicalisation or terrorism, always append a trusted_adult_prompt. flag_for_review signals a system-side moderation flag.

column3: has_grief_loss = true; needs_emotional_support = true
column6: G4 action is TRANSFORM. G3_MOD will include empathetic_tone and emotional_support_required. Acknowledge feelings first before any information. If self-harm signals co-present, severity escalates to SV3. If safeguarding_concern present, append trusted_adult_prompt.

column3: high_complexity_for_5_8 = true
column6: G4 action is TRANSFORM. Simplify language and concept level for 5-8 band. If emotional co-signals present, apply empathetic_tone from G3_MOD. No block unless other GLs also fire.

column3: biased_group_language = true; forbid_negative_group_language = true
column6: G4 action is TRANSFORM or BLOCK depending on severity. Do not reproduce or amplify negative group language. Reframe to factual or neutral framing. If hate language is severe, treat as SV3 + BLOCK.

column3: factual_civic_religious = true
column6: G4 action is ALLOW or TRANSFORM. Purely factual civic/religious questions are generally safe (SV0-SV1). Do not conflate factual description with comparative or loaded framing. Curiosity_invite applies unless overridden.

column3: has_grooming_indicator = true; needs_safeguarding_line = true
column6: G4 action is BLOCK (Hard). G3_SV = SV3. G3_MOD = {no_curiosity_invite, zero_engagement, safeguarding_concern}. Do not engage with premise. Do not explain why. Append ONLY the minimal policy-approved trusted_adult_prompt (if permitted by deployment policy). No curiosity_invite. No alternate question.

column3: has_unsafe_content = true; needs_immediate_block = true
column6: G4 action is BLOCK (Hard / Absolute). G3_SV = SV3. G3_MOD = {no_curiosity_invite, no_content_engagement, no_reason_given, zero_engagement}. Hardest block in the system. No response content whatsoever. No reason. No alternate question. No trusted_adult_prompt in response layer (system escalation only).

column3: has_coercive_control = true; needs_safeguarding_line = true
column6: G4 action is TRANSFORM or BLOCK + ESCALATE. G3_SV = SV3. G3_MOD includes empathetic_tone. Do not reinforce the controlling dynamic. Acknowledge child's safety. Append trusted_adult_prompt. Escalate at system layer if escalate modifier present.

column3: has_vuln_exploitation = true; needs_safeguarding_line = true
column6: G4 action is BLOCK + ESCALATE. G3_SV = SV3. G3_MOD includes safeguarding_concern. Prioritise child safety. Do not engage with the exploitative dynamic. Append trusted_adult_prompt. System-layer escalation always triggered.

column1: BLOCK F — PROMPT AUTHORING RULESPurpose: This block governs how prompts in Column O must be authored to ensure faithful translation of G4 gate outputs into LLM-readable instructions. Every prompt MUST respect and enforce the gate logic without softening, bypassing, or paraphrasing around it.

column3: Rule Description
column6: Corrective Action

column3: Every prompt must faithfully reflect the G4 Action, Base Ending, and Response Style without softening, omitting, or paraphrasing. The prompt is an instruction contract — not a creative rewrite.
column5: Prompt says 'respond helpfully' when G4 = BLOCK; prompt omits key modifier tags; prompt reframes hard block as a soft redirect.
column6: Re-author prompt to match G4 output exactly. Use the Gate 4 Full Description as the primary source.

column3: When G3_MOD includes clarification_required, the Turn 1 prompt MUST output ONLY one safe clarification question. No substantive answer, no topic content, no explanation, no redirect question is permitted in Turn 1.
column5: Prompt generates a substantive answer in Turn 1 despite clarification_required being set. Prompt includes 'explain that...' or 'answer safely...' alongside the clarification ask.
column6: Strip all content from Turn 1. Prompt must contain ONLY: [Age] [G1] [G2: AMBIGUOUS_RISK] [G3: SV2 | clarification_required] [G4: TRANSFORM(hold)] + instruction to ask exactly one safe clarification question and stop.

column3: When no_content_engagement is in G3_MOD, the prompt MUST prohibit ANY topic-related content, explanation, reason-giving, or redirect question. The only permitted output is a minimal block acknowledgment (if any) or complete silence.
column5: Prompt says 'tell the child why we always redirect' or 'ask them a safe question instead' or 'explain that this is not something we discuss' — all of which engage with the topic.
column6: Remove all explanatory content. Prompt must explicitly say: Do NOT explain, do NOT redirect with a question, do NOT acknowledge the topic. If any text is permitted, it must be a single pre-approved minimal block phrase only.

column3: When no_curiosity_invite is in G3_MOD, the prompt MUST NOT include any ending question, curiosity prompt, or follow-up invitation of any kind. The response must end cleanly without an open question.
column4: All rows where G3_MOD contains no_curiosity_invite
column5: Prompt ends with 'Question: How does X work?' or 'What do you think about Y?' despite no_curiosity_invite being set.
column6: Remove all ending questions from the prompt. Final sentence must be a statement, not a question. Do not add any variation of 'What do you think?' or 'Can you tell me more?'.

column2: Age Band and Length Constraint are Mandatory
column3: Every prompt MUST declare the child's age band explicitly (e.g., 5-8 years) and instruct the LLM to keep the response to 5 lines or less. These are non-negotiable baseline requirements for all prompt types.
column5: Prompt omits age band declaration; prompt does not specify line limit; prompt uses 'keep it simple' without specifying the exact constraint.
column6: Add [Age: 5-8] tag at start of every prompt. Add explicit instruction 'Respond in 5 lines or less' before any content instruction.

column1: BLOCK G — HARD BLOCK PROMPT TEMPLATEPurpose: Provides standardised, pre-approved prompt templates for the two hardest block types: (1) DANGEROUS / no_content_engagement and (2) clarification_required HOLD prompts. These templates are the ONLY approved format for these cases. Do not deviate or paraphrase.

column3: Approved Prompt Template
column5: What MUST NOT Appear in Output

column2: G2 = DANGEROUS | G3_MOD includes no_content_engagement | G4 = BLOCK
column3: [Age: 5-8 | G1: {G1_VALUE} | G2: DANGEROUS | G3: SV3 | no_curiosity_invite, no_content_engagement | G4: BLOCK] You are a safe, caring helper for a 5-year-old child. This question asks about something that could be dangerous. Do NOT explain how or why. Do NOT engage with the topic. Do NOT ask a follow-up question. Respond in 5 lines or less by telling the child kindly that this is something you cannot help with, and they should ask a grown-up if they have questions.
column4: Must not explain mechanism. Must not name the topic. Must not redirect with a question. Must end with grown-up referral only.
column5: Any explanation of why the topic is dangerous; any redirect question; any alternate topic suggestion; any use of the word 'explode', 'fire', 'harm', or the topic noun.

column2: G2 = AMBIGUOUS_RISK | G3_MOD includes clarification_required | G4 = TRANSFORM (hold)
column3: [Age: 5-8 | G1: {G1_VALUE} | G2: AMBIGUOUS_RISK | G3: SV2 | clarification_required, no_curiosity_invite | G4: TRANSFORM (hold)] You are a safe, caring helper for a 5-year-old child. This question could mean different things. Before answering, you MUST ask exactly ONE safe clarification question to understand what the child means. Do NOT answer the question. Do NOT provide any information about the topic. Respond in 1 sentence only with your clarification question.
column4: Turn 1 output = exactly 1 clarification question. No content answer. No topic engagement. Question must be safe regardless of what child means.
column5: Any substantive answer; any topic explanation; any attempt to 'safely' address the topic; any follow-up question after clarification ask; any redirect.

column1: BLOCK H — PROMPT COMPLIANCE CHECKLISTPurpose: Before any prompt is written into Column O, the author must verify ALL items on this checklist. A prompt may only be marked 'approved' if every checklist item passes. Use this as a pre-submission gate.

column3: How to Verify

column2: Age band is declared in prompt header
column3: Look for [Age: 5-8] or equivalent explicit tag at the start of the prompt string.
column5: No age tag present, or age is implied rather than stated explicitly.CHK-02
column6: G1, G2, G3, G4 values are all present in prompt header
column7: Verify prompt header contains: [G1: X | G2: X | G3: SVx | modifiers | G4: ACTION]
column9: Any gate value missing or abbreviated without modifier tags.CHK-03
column11: Search for 'Respond in 5 lines or less' or equivalent explicit instruction in the prompt body.
column13: Line limit omitted or phrased as 'keep it brief' without specifying the number.

column2: G1, G2, G3, G4 values are all present in prompt header
column3: Verify prompt header contains: [G1: X | G2: X | G3: SVx | modifiers | G4: ACTION]
column5: Any gate value missing or abbreviated without modifier tags.CHK-03
column6: 5-line limit instruction is present
column7: Search for 'Respond in 5 lines or less' or equivalent explicit instruction in the prompt body.
column9: Line limit omitted or phrased as 'keep it brief' without specifying the number.

column3: Search for 'Respond in 5 lines or less' or equivalent explicit instruction in the prompt body.
column5: Line limit omitted or phrased as 'keep it brief' without specifying the number.

column2: If clarification_required is in G3_MOD, Turn 1 contains ONLY the clarification question
column3: Read Turn 1 instruction in the prompt. Confirm it outputs ONLY a single question. No answer, no content.
column5: Turn 1 contains any substantive content, explanation, or redirect alongside or after the clarification question.CHK-05
column6: If no_content_engagement is in G3_MOD, prompt contains NO topic content
column7: Scan prompt for topic-related words, explanations, reasons, or redirect questions. Any presence = FAIL.
column9: Prompt contains any explanation of the topic, any mechanism description, any reason-giving, or any redirect question related to the blocked topic.CHK-06
column10: If no_curiosity_invite is in G3_MOD, prompt ends without a question
column11: Read the final sentence of the response instruction. Confirm it is a statement, not a question.
column12: All rows with no_curiosity_invite in G3_MOD
column13: Final instruction ends with a question mark or includes any invitation for further questioning.CHK-07
column14: G4 Action matches the actual instruction in the prompt body
column15: If G4 = BLOCK, prompt must say block/do not answer. If G4 = ALLOW, prompt invites a full answer. If G4 = TRANSFORM, prompt asks for reframing.
column17: G4 = BLOCK but prompt says 'answer helpfully'; G4 = ALLOW but prompt adds restrictions not warranted by gate.CHK-08
column18: Prompt does not introduce content, instructions, or framing not supported by gate outputs
column19: Review prompt for any added language, explanations, caveats, or encouragements not derivable from G1-G4 outputs.
column21: Prompt adds extra helpful phrasing, unsolicited explanations, or softens block language using 'but you can always ask' type redirects.

column2: If no_content_engagement is in G3_MOD, prompt contains NO topic content
column3: Scan prompt for topic-related words, explanations, reasons, or redirect questions. Any presence = FAIL.
column5: Prompt contains any explanation of the topic, any mechanism description, any reason-giving, or any redirect question related to the blocked topic.CHK-06
column6: If no_curiosity_invite is in G3_MOD, prompt ends without a question
column7: Read the final sentence of the response instruction. Confirm it is a statement, not a question.
column8: All rows with no_curiosity_invite in G3_MOD
column9: Final instruction ends with a question mark or includes any invitation for further questioning.CHK-07
column10: G4 Action matches the actual instruction in the prompt body
column11: If G4 = BLOCK, prompt must instruct 'do not answer'. If G4 = ALLOW, prompt invites a full answer. If G4 = TRANSFORM, prompt asks for reframing.
column13: G4 = BLOCK but prompt says 'answer helpfully'; G4 = ALLOW but prompt adds restrictions not supported by gate.CHK-08
column14: Prompt does not introduce content, framing, or language not supported by gate outputs
column15: Review prompt for any added caveats, helpful phrasing, or redirects not derivable from G1-G4 outputs.
column17: Prompt softens block language with 'but you can always ask a grown-up'; adds unsolicited encouragement; introduces topic content not triggered by gate logic.

column2: If no_curiosity_invite is in G3_MOD, prompt ends without a question
column3: Read the final sentence of the response instruction. Confirm it is a statement, not a question.
column4: All rows with no_curiosity_invite in G3_MOD
column5: Final instruction ends with a question mark or includes any invitation for further questioning.CHK-07
column6: G4 Action matches the actual instruction in the prompt body
column7: If G4 = BLOCK, prompt must say 'do not answer'. If G4 = ALLOW, prompt invites a full answer. If G4 = TRANSFORM, prompt asks for reframing.
column9: G4 = BLOCK but prompt says 'answer helpfully'; G4 = ALLOW but prompt adds restrictions not supported by gate.CHK-08
column10: Prompt does not introduce content, framing, or language not supported by gate outputs
column11: Review prompt for any added caveats, helpful phrasing, or redirects not derivable from G1-G4 outputs.
column13: Prompt softens block language with 'but you can always ask a grown-up'; adds unsolicited encouragement; introduces topic content not triggered by gate logic.

column2: G4 Action matches the actual instruction in the prompt body
column3: If G4 = BLOCK, prompt must say 'do not answer'. If G4 = ALLOW, prompt invites a full answer. If G4 = TRANSFORM, prompt asks for reframing.
column5: G4 = BLOCK but prompt says 'answer helpfully'; G4 = ALLOW but prompt adds restrictions not supported by gate.CHK-08
